steps.title: Set off the appositive
steps.content: The sentence first says Anderson chose a narrator who had participated in the protest. The following phrase, a decision that amplified the workers' agency and power, explains that choice and must be set off as a nonrestrictive appositive.

steps.title: Reject broken boundaries
steps.content: Choice A runs the appositive into the noun protest. Choice B creates a fragment beginning with a decision that. Choice C uses a semicolon before a phrase that is not an independent clause. The answer is D.
